label: Corticostriatal (motor)
description: Sensorimotor frontal cortex drives the putamen, the motor input nucleus of the basal ganglia.

sources.citation: Alexander GE, DeLong MR, Strick PL (1986). Parallel organization of functionally segregated circuits linking basal ganglia and cortex. Annu Rev Neurosci 9:357-381.
Kemp JM, Powell TPS (1971). The cortico-striate projection in the monkey. Brain 94(3):525-546.

label: Corticostriatal (motor)
description: Sensorimotor frontal cortex drives the putamen, the motor input nucleus of the basal ganglia.

sources.citation: Alexander GE, DeLong MR, Strick PL (1986). Parallel organization of functionally segregated circuits linking basal ganglia and cortex. Annu Rev Neurosci 9:357-381.
Kemp JM, Powell TPS (1971). The cortico-striate projection in the monkey. Brain 94(3):525-546.

label: Corticostriatal (associative)
description: Prefrontal cortex drives the caudate (associative striatum).

sources.citation: Alexander GE, DeLong MR, Strick PL (1986). Parallel organization of functionally segregated circuits linking basal ganglia and cortex. Annu Rev Neurosci 9:357-381.
Kemp JM, Powell TPS (1971). The cortico-striate projection in the monkey. Brain 94(3):525-546.

Kemp JM, Powell TPS (1971). The cortico-striate projection in the monkey. Brain 94(3):525-546.

label: Corticostriatal (parietal)
description: Posterior parietal association cortex projects to the caudate.

sources.citation: Kemp JM, Powell TPS (1971). The cortico-striate projection in the monkey. Brain 94(3):525-546.

label: Corticostriatal (parietal)
description: Posterior parietal association cortex projects to the caudate.

sources.citation: Kemp JM, Powell TPS (1971). The cortico-striate projection in the monkey. Brain 94(3):525-546.

label: Corticostriatal (temporal)
description: Temporal association cortex projects to the striatum.

sources.citation: Kemp JM, Powell TPS (1971). The cortico-striate projection in the monkey. Brain 94(3):525-546.

label: Corticostriatal (temporal)
description: Temporal association cortex projects to the striatum.

sources.citation: Kemp JM, Powell TPS (1971). The cortico-striate projection in the monkey. Brain 94(3):525-546.

label: Hyperdirect (corticosubthalamic)
description: Cortex excites the subthalamic nucleus directly, the fast 'hyperdirect' brake on movement.

sources.citation: Nambu A, Tokuno H, Takada M (2002). Functional significance of the cortico-subthalamo-pallidal 'hyperdirect' pathway. Neurosci Res 43(2):111-117.

label: Hyperdirect (corticosubthalamic)
description: Cortex excites the subthalamic nucleus directly, the fast 'hyperdirect' brake on movement.

sources.citation: Nambu A, Tokuno H, Takada M (2002). Functional significance of the cortico-subthalamo-pallidal 'hyperdirect' pathway. Neurosci Res 43(2):111-117.

label: Striatopallidal (direct)
description: Direct-pathway striatal neurons inhibit the internal pallidum, releasing (disinhibiting) the thalamus.

sources.citation: Albin RL, Young AB, Penney JB (1989). The functional anatomy of basal ganglia disorders. Trends Neurosci 12(10):366-375.
Smith Y, Bevan MD, Shink E, Bolam JP (1998). Microcircuitry of the direct and indirect pathways of the basal ganglia. Neuroscience 86(2):353-387.

label: Striatopallidal (direct)
description: Direct-pathway striatal neurons inhibit the internal pallidum, releasing (disinhibiting) the thalamus.

label: Striatopallidal (direct)
description: Caudate direct-pathway output to the internal pallidum.

description: Caudate direct-pathway output to the internal pallidum.

label: Striatonigral (direct)
description: Direct-pathway striatal output to the substantia nigra pars reticulata.

label: Striatonigral (direct)
description: Caudate direct-pathway output to the substantia nigra.

label: Pallidosubthalamic (indirect)
description: External pallidum inhibits the STN in the indirect pathway.

description: External pallidum inhibits the STN in the indirect pathway.

Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Subthalamopallidal
description: The STN excites the pallidum, amplifying basal-ganglia output (indirect/hyperdirect pathways).

sources.citation: Albin RL, Young AB, Penney JB (1989). The functional anatomy of basal ganglia disorders. Trends Neurosci 12(10):366-375.
Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Subthalamopallidal
description: The STN excites the pallidum, amplifying basal-ganglia output (indirect/hyperdirect pathways).

sources.citation: Albin RL, Young AB, Penney JB (1989). The functional anatomy of basal ganglia disorders. Trends Neurosci 12(10):366-375.
Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Nigrostriatal
description: Substantia nigra pars compacta dopamine sets the balance between the direct and indirect striatal pathways.

sources.citation: DeLong MR (1990). Primate models of movement disorders of basal ganglia origin. Trends Neurosci 13(7):281-285.
Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

description: Substantia nigra pars compacta dopamine sets the balance between the direct and indirect striatal pathways.

label: Nigrostriatal
description: Dopaminergic modulation of the caudate.

sources.citation: DeLong MR (1990). Primate models of movement disorders of basal ganglia origin. Trends Neurosci 13(7):281-285.
Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Nigrostriatal
description: Dopaminergic modulation of the caudate.

Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Pallidothalamic
description: The internal pallidum tonically inhibits the motor thalamus, the output gate of the loop.

sources.citation: Alexander GE, DeLong MR, Strick PL (1986). Parallel organization of functionally segregated circuits linking basal ganglia and cortex. Annu Rev Neurosci 9:357-381.
Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Pallidothalamic
description: The internal pallidum tonically inhibits the motor thalamus, the output gate of the loop.

sources.citation: Alexander GE, DeLong MR, Strick PL (1986). Parallel organization of functionally segregated circuits linking basal ganglia and cortex. Annu Rev Neurosci 9:357-381.
Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Nigrothalamic
description: Substantia nigra pars reticulata inhibitory output to the thalamus.

sources.citation: Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Nigrothalamic
description: Substantia nigra pars reticulata inhibitory output to the thalamus.

sources.citation: Parent A, Hazrati LN (1995). Functional anatomy of the basal ganglia. Brain Res Rev 20(1):91-154.

label: Thalamocortical
description: Motor thalamus excites frontal cortex, closing the cortico-basal-ganglia-thalamo-cortical loop.

sources.citation: Alexander GE, DeLong MR, Strick PL (1986). Parallel organization of functionally segregated circuits linking basal ganglia and cortex. Annu Rev Neurosci 9:357-381.

label: Thalamocortical
description: Motor thalamus excites frontal cortex, closing the cortico-basal-ganglia-thalamo-cortical loop.

sources.citation: Alexander GE, DeLong MR, Strick PL (1986). Parallel organization of functionally segregated circuits linking basal ganglia and cortex. Annu Rev Neurosci 9:357-381.

label: Corticothalamic (visual)
description: Occipital (visual) cortex reciprocally connects with the thalamus (pulvinar / lateral geniculate).

sources.citation: Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Corticothalamic (visual)
description: Occipital (visual) cortex reciprocally connects with the thalamus (pulvinar / lateral geniculate).

sources.citation: Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Corticopontine
description: Cortex projects to the pontine nuclei (brainstem), the first leg of the cortico-ponto-cerebellar route.

sources.citation: Middleton FA, Strick PL (2000). Basal ganglia and cerebellar loops: motor and cognitive circuits. Brain Res Rev 31(2-3):236-250.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Corticopontine
description: Cortex projects to the pontine nuclei (brainstem), the first leg of the cortico-ponto-cerebellar route.

sources.citation: Middleton FA, Strick PL (2000). Basal ganglia and cerebellar loops: motor and cognitive circuits. Brain Res Rev 31(2-3):236-250.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Pontocerebellar (mossy fibers)
description: Pontine nuclei send mossy fibers to the cerebellar cortex.

sources.citation: Middleton FA, Strick PL (2000). Basal ganglia and cerebellar loops: motor and cognitive circuits. Brain Res Rev 31(2-3):236-250.

label: Cerebellothalamic (dentatothalamic)
description: Deep cerebellar nuclei drive the motor thalamus, feeding the cerebellar loop back to cortex.

sources.citation: Middleton FA, Strick PL (2000). Basal ganglia and cerebellar loops: motor and cognitive circuits. Brain Res Rev 31(2-3):236-250.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Cerebellothalamic (dentatothalamic)
description: Deep cerebellar nuclei drive the motor thalamus, feeding the cerebellar loop back to cortex.

sources.citation: Middleton FA, Strick PL (2000). Basal ganglia and cerebellar loops: motor and cognitive circuits. Brain Res Rev 31(2-3):236-250.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Perforant path
description: Entorhinal (medial temporal) cortex drives the hippocampus via the perforant path.

sources.citation: Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Perforant path
description: Entorhinal (medial temporal) cortex drives the hippocampus via the perforant path.

sources.citation: Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Fornix
description: Major hippocampal output, arching via the fornix to the mammillary bodies of the hypothalamus (Papez circuit).

sources.citation: Papez JW (1937). A proposed mechanism of emotion. Arch Neurol Psychiatry 38(4):725-743.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Fornix
description: Major hippocampal output, arching via the fornix to the mammillary bodies of the hypothalamus (Papez circuit).

sources.citation: Papez JW (1937). A proposed mechanism of emotion. Arch Neurol Psychiatry 38(4):725-743.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Mammillothalamic tract
description: Mammillary bodies to the anterior thalamus, continuing the Papez circuit.

sources.citation: Papez JW (1937). A proposed mechanism of emotion. Arch Neurol Psychiatry 38(4):725-743.

label: Mammillothalamic tract
description: Mammillary bodies to the anterior thalamus, continuing the Papez circuit.

sources.citation: Papez JW (1937). A proposed mechanism of emotion. Arch Neurol Psychiatry 38(4):725-743.

label: Corpus callosum (frontal)
description: Homotopic callosal fibers linking the two frontal lobes.

sources.citation: Aboitiz F, Scheibel AB, Fisher RS, Zaidel E (1992). Fiber composition of the human corpus callosum. Brain Res 598(1-2):143-153.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Corpus callosum (parietal)
description: Homotopic callosal fibers linking the two parietal lobes.

sources.citation: Aboitiz F, Scheibel AB, Fisher RS, Zaidel E (1992). Fiber composition of the human corpus callosum. Brain Res 598(1-2):143-153.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Corpus callosum (splenium / occipital)
description: Splenial callosal fibers linking the two occipital lobes.

sources.citation: Aboitiz F, Scheibel AB, Fisher RS, Zaidel E (1992). Fiber composition of the human corpus callosum. Brain Res 598(1-2):143-153.
Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.

label: Anterior commissure
description: Older commissure linking the temporal lobes (and olfactory structures).

sources.citation: Schmahmann JD, Pandya DN (2006). Fiber Pathways of the Brain. Oxford University Press.
